title: Epic: ISO Builder Production Readiness
description:
**Epic: ISO Builder Production Readiness**

Make the projectbluefin/iso builder production-ready with reliable automated builds and streamlined promotion workflows.

**Current Status (70% complete):**
- ✅ LTS-HWE: Building successfully
- ❌ Stable: Build failures since Feb 1, 2026 (CRITICAL)
- ❌ LTS non-HWE: Intentionally disabled (no longer supported)
- ✅ Infrastructure: Workflows, R2 storage, promotion pipeline exist
- ⚠️ Promotion workflow needs streamlining

**Scope:**
- Fix current Stable build failures (unblock production)
- Streamline promotion workflow (testing → production)
- Ensure LTS non-HWE stays disabled permanently
- Document operational procedures

**Repositories affected:**
- [iso] All ISO builder improvements
- [docs] ISO operational runbooks

**Related upstream issues:**
- projectbluefin/iso#21 - Bluefin branding missing (CLOSED - fixed)
- Recent Stable build failures (needs investigation)

**Out of scope:**
- Dakota installer (separate project)
- LTS Anaconda fixes (non-HWE intentionally disabled)
- Automated VM testing (infrastructure not ready)
- First-boot/onboarding experience

title: [iso] Ensure LTS non-HWE stays disabled
description:
Confirm LTS workflow is permanently disabled and will not publish new ISOs.

**Background:**
- LTS non-HWE is no longer supported
- Workflow intentionally disabled
- Existing ISOs in R2 bucket should remain available
- Only LTS-HWE variant continues

**Actions:**
1. Verify build-iso-lts.yml workflow is disabled/commented
2. Add code comment explaining permanent disable
3. Update README to clarify only LTS-HWE supported
4. Ensure existing LTS ISOs remain in R2 bucket (don't delete)
5. Document in AGENTS.md why LTS non-HWE is disabled

**Deliverable:**
- LTS workflow confirmed permanently disabled
- Documentation updated across repo
- No accidental LTS ISO builds possible

**Test:** Workflow cannot be triggered, documentation is clear

title: Epic: Flatpak Ecosystem Expansion
description: Expand flatpak overrides for common apps (Steam, Discord, OBS), create appimage repository for non-flatpak apps (#199), maintain Bazaar curated list, and improve flatpak-first workflow. Build on strong foundation.

title: Epic: Enhanced Privacy & Security Configuration
description: Expand privacy-focused defaults across Firefox, Flatpak apps, and system-level security hardening. Current Firefox config is minimal (3 lines). Opportunity to add tracking protection, telemetry controls, hardware security integration, and privacy-preserving defaults.
close_reason: Epic deleted - Firefox is out of scope, no upstream privacy/security initiatives found

title: Epic: AI/ML Integration (Bluespeed)
description: Build comprehensive AI/ML tooling and integration. Ship goose and linux-mcp-server (#156), expand ai-tools.Brewfile, integrate with system workflows, and create curated AI app recommendations. Addresses #100.

title: Epic: Automation & CI/CD Enhancement
description: Improve build automation with changelog generator 2.0 (#181), brew upstream-triggered builds (#123), rebase-helper refactor (#120), SBOM checks (#144), and testing branch support (#130). Strengthen already robust GHA infrastructure.

title: [iso] Verify R2 secrets and test promotion workflow
description:
Verify CloudFlare R2 secrets are valid and test current promotion workflow.

**Secrets to verify (6 configured):**
- Testing bucket: R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID_2025, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY_2025, R2_ENDPOINT_2025
- Production bucket: R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID_PRODTEST, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY_PRODTEST, R2_ENDPOINT_PRODTEST

**Actions:**
1. Test manual promotion workflow (dry-run mode)
2. Verify ISOs upload to testing bucket successfully
3. Test promotion from testing → production
4. Identify pain points in current promotion process
5. Document what works and what doesn't

**Deliverable:**
- Secrets verified working
- Assessment of current promotion workflow state
- List of improvements needed

**Test:** Successful dry-run promotion workflow execution

title: Epic: Setup Hooks Infrastructure Activation
description: Leverage the existing setup hooks infrastructure (privileged/system/user) to automate first-boot experiences, hardware detection, development environment setup, and configuration templates. Infrastructure exists but no hooks are deployed.

title: Epic: CLI Bling & Terminal Excellence
description: Enhance terminal experience with new bling (#71), prettier fastfetch (#176), improved bbrew navigation (#126), better bluefin-cli toggle functionality (#164), and refined MOTD system. Build on solid CLI foundation with 20+ modern tools.

title: Epic: Documentation & Knowledge Base
description: Centralize CONTRIBUTING.md (#91), create architecture documentation, add hook development guide, improve inline documentation, document Brewfile contribution process, and make documentation.desktop open PDF (#185).

title: Epic: Testing & Quality Infrastructure
description: Establish comprehensive testing infrastructure including OpenQA investigation (#159), testing branches (#163), automated validation for JSON/YAML, shell script linting, and integration tests. Currently no visible test infrastructure.

title: [iso] Fix Stable ISO build failures
description:
Investigate and fix Stable ISO build failures occurring since Feb 1, 2026.

**Failure pattern:**
- Last success: Before Feb 1, 2026
- Recent failures: Feb 3, Feb 1, Jan 29, 2026
- Status: Consistently failing on scheduled builds
- Impact: No new Stable ISOs being produced

**Investigation steps:**
1. Review workflow logs for recent failures
2. Check if bluefin:stable image is pulling correctly
3. Verify titanoboa action compatibility
4. Check runner resource constraints
5. Compare with working LTS-HWE workflow

**Deliverable:** 
- Root cause identified and documented
- Fix implemented and tested
- Successful Stable ISO build

**Test:** Trigger manual Stable build, verify success
notes:
Root cause identified: anaconda-webui package conflict with generic-logos vs fedora-logos.

Fix implemented:
- Added --allowerasing to dnf install command
- Made ublue-guest-user.service disable non-fatal (|| true)

PR created: castrojo/iso fix/stable-anaconda-webui-conflict → projectbluefin/iso

Waiting for PR merge and build validation.

title: [iso] Create ISO operational runbook
description:
Document operational procedures for ISO management.

**Runbook sections:**

1. **Promotion Procedures**
   - Step-by-step promotion workflow
   - How to verify ISO quality before promoting
   - What to check before promotion
   - When to promote (schedule/triggers)

2. **Troubleshooting Guide**
   - Common build failures and fixes
   - Stable build failure patterns
   - How to investigate workflow failures

3. **R2 Bucket Management**
   - Bucket structure and naming conventions
   - How to access and browse buckets
   - ISO retention/cleanup policies

4. **Emergency Procedures**
   - How to hotfix critical build issues
   - Who to contact for R2 access issues
   - Escalation paths

**Deliverable:**
- RUNBOOK.md in projectbluefin/iso repository
- Linked from README and AGENTS.md
- Reviewed by maintainers

**Success criteria:** New maintainer can follow runbooks without prior knowledge

title: Epic: GNOME Desktop Polish
description: Enhance GNOME integration with gradia screenshot tool (#127), improved emoji picker (#125), show details menu in app grid (#182), gnome-help implementation (#171), and refined desktop experience. Build on already excellent GNOME configuration.

title: Epic: Developer Experience Modernization
description:
**Epic: Developer Experience Configuration Layer**

Create a shared DX configuration layer in bluefin-common to centralize developer experience configs across all Bluefin variants (bluefin, bluefin-lts, future variants). Supports the DX-less DX transition where container/virtualization tools move to the base image.

**Scope:**
- Migrate DX configuration files from ublue-os/bluefin to projectbluefin/common
- Enable ublue-os/bluefin-lts to consume shared DX configs
- Support DX-less DX architecture (issue ublue-os/bluefin#3879)
- Configuration only: package installation scripts remain variant-specific

**What moves to common (config files):**
- VSCode default settings and theme
- DX-specific Just recipes
- DX-specific flatpak overrides
- DX documentation and MOTD tips
- IDE configuration templates

**What stays in variant repos (build scripts):**
- Package installation (different repos: Fedora vs CentOS)
- Service enablement (may differ between variants)
- User group management (bluefin vs LTS differences)
- Setup hooks (need variant versioning)

**Repositories affected:**
- [common] New system_files/dx/ configuration layer
- [bluefin] Consume DX configs from common
- [bluefin-lts] Consume DX configs from common
- [docs] Document DX configuration architecture

**Related upstream issues:**
- ublue-os/bluefin#3879 - Remove need for dedicated DX image (DX-less DX)
- ublue-os/bluefin#3724 - Add git-credential-manager-core
- ublue-os/bluefin#3814 - GUI app for bootc features
- ublue-os/bluefin#3883 - Docker NVML errors
- ublue-os/bluefin#3293 - NVIDIA GPU not working after updates
- ublue-os/bluefin-lts#947 - Cockpit missing on DX images (closed)
- ublue-os/bluefin-lts#923 - zsh missing from DX (closed)
- projectbluefin/common#155 - Curated mise onboarding (closed)

**Future enhancements (out of scope for this epic):**
- Mise integration and onboarding
- Enhanced IDE configurations (JetBrains, Neovim, Helix, Emacs)
- Kubernetes tooling (k3s, kind)
- Lima integration for VM/container bridge
- Visual "dev mode engaged" indicator

title: [iso] Streamline promotion workflow
description:
Make testing → production promotion easier and safer.

**Current gaps identified:**
- Manual workflow_dispatch with multiple parameters
- Unclear what to promote and when
- No safety checks or validation

**Improvements to implement:**
1. Simplify workflow inputs (reduce manual parameters)
2. Add safety checks (verify ISO exists before promoting)
3. Better logging/output (what was promoted, when, by whom)
4. Improve dry-run mode output
5. Document promotion process step-by-step

**Questions to answer:**
- Per-variant promotion or all-at-once?
- How often to promote (weekly/monthly/on-demand)?
- What quality checks before promotion?

**Deliverable:**
- Enhanced promotion workflow
- Clear promotion procedures
- Documented process

**Test:** Promotion workflow is easier to use and safer

title: Epic: Hardware Support & Optimization
description: Expand hardware-specific optimizations beyond excellent Framework/gaming support. Add more color profiles, optimize power management, expand udev rules for emerging hardware, and improve detection automation via setup hooks.
